title: Willoughbya aspera

The plant has been traditionally used by Aboriginal Australians for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its use in treating various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Willoughbya aspera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Willoughbya aspera is used in various herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin irritations, although the plant's use is not well-documented in modern scientific literature.
